title: ranunculus longicaulis var. geniculatus
intro: Ranunculus longicaulis var. geniculatus, also known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the ranunculus genus, characterized by its shiny, yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of ranunculus longicaulis var. geniculatus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, arthritis, and digestive issues, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
